name: Caramel Brownies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour a 9 x 13 baking pan. Combine caramels and evaporated milk in the top of a double boiler over low heat. Cover and simmer until caramels are melted, stirring occasionally. Set aside, keeping warm.
2. Combine German sweet chocolate and butter in a 2-quart saucepan. Place over low heat, stirring occasionally until melted. Remove from heat and cool to room temperature.
3. Beat eggs with an electric mixer on high speed until foamy. Gradually add sugar, beating until the mixture is thick and lemon-colored.
4. Sift together flour, baking powder, and salt. Add egg mixture gradually, mixing well. Blend in the cooled chocolate mixture and vanilla.
5. Spread half of the batter into the prepared pan. Bake for 6 minutes. Remove from the oven and spread the caramel mixture carefully over the baked layer. Sprinkle with chocolate chips.
6. Stir the chopped walnuts into the remaining chocolate batter. Spread the batter by spoonfuls over the caramel layer. Sprinkle with the remaining nuts.
7. Bake for 20 minutes. Cool in the pan on a rack. Refrigerate before cutting into bars or squares. These brownies are very difficult to cut if not chilled first.
